creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Älven from comment #7)

The main reason is FreeBSD commit messages have a hard 72-column limit, and convention for new ports is:

category/port: COMMENT (condensed if necessary)

pkg-descr

So 80 won't work for new ports.  There isn't a strong motive to change it for existing ports in my view.  If you do, it should probably be done in a separate commit with only cosmetic changes, so that these changes don't clutter git diffs for more interesting changes.  But I wouldn't do a commit just to reduce pkg-descr from 80 columns to 70.  That would just be noise in the git log.  If a Makefile doesn't pass portlint, or pkg-descr is being rewritten, that would be the time to reformat to 70 cols.
